1→// Copyright (C) NHR@FAU, University Erlangen-Nuremberg.
     2→// All rights reserved. This file is part of cc-backend.
     3→// Use of this source code is governed by a MIT-style
     4→// license that can be found in the LICENSE file.
     5→
     6→package api
     7→
     8→import (
     9→	"bufio"
    10→	"database/sql"
    11→	"encoding/json"
    12→	"errors"
    13→	"fmt"
    14→	"net/http"
    15→	"strconv"
    16→	"strings"
    17→	"sync"
    18→	"time"
    19→
    20→	"github.com/ClusterCockpit/cc-backend/internal/archiver"
    21→	"github.com/ClusterCockpit/cc-backend/internal/config"
    22→	"github.com/ClusterCockpit/cc-backend/internal/graph"
    23→	"github.com/ClusterCockpit/cc-backend/internal/graph/model"
    24→	"github.com/ClusterCockpit/cc-backend/internal/importer"
    25→	"github.com/ClusterCockpit/cc-backend/internal/metricdispatch"
    26→	"github.com/ClusterCockpit/cc-backend/internal/repository"
    27→	"github.com/ClusterCockpit/cc-backend/pkg/archive"
    28→	cclog "github.com/ClusterCockpit/cc-lib/v2/ccLogger"
    29→	"github.com/ClusterCockpit/cc-lib/v2/schema"
    30→	"github.com/go-chi/chi/v5"
    31→)
    32→
    33→const (
    34→	// secondsPerDay is the number of seconds in 24 hours.
    35→	// Used for duplicate job detection within a day window.
    36→	secondsPerDay = 86400
    37→)
    38→
    39→// StopJobAPIRequest model
    40→type StopJobAPIRequest struct {
    41→	JobID     *int64          `json:"jobId" example:"123000"`
    42→	Cluster   *string         `json:"cluster" example:"fritz"`
    43→	StartTime *int64          `json:"startTime" example:"1649723812"`
    44→	State     schema.JobState `json:"jobState" validate:"required" example:"completed"`
    45→	StopTime  int64           `json:"stopTime" validate:"required" example:"1649763839"`
    46→}
    47→
    48→// DeleteJobAPIRequest model
    49→type DeleteJobAPIRequest struct {
    50→	JobID     *int64  `json:"jobId" validate:"required" example:"123000"` // Cluster Job ID of job
    51→	Cluster   *string `json:"cluster" example:"fritz"`                    // Cluster of job
    52→	StartTime *int64  `json:"startTime" example:"1649723812"`             // Start Time of job as epoch
    53→}
    54→
    55→// GetJobsAPIResponse model
    56→type GetJobsAPIResponse struct {
    57→	Jobs  []*schema.Job `json:"jobs"`  // Array of jobs
    58→	Items int           `json:"items"` // Number of jobs returned
    59→	Page  int           `json:"page"`  // Page id returned
    60→}
    61→
    62→// APITag model
    63→type APITag struct {
    64→	// Tag Type
    65→	Type  string `json:"type" example:"Debug"`
    66→	Name  string `json:"name" example:"Testjob"` // Tag Name
    67→	Scope string `json:"scope" example:"global"` // Tag Scope for Frontend Display
    68→}
    69→
    70→type EditMetaRequest struct {
    71→	Key   string `json:"key" example:"jobScript"`
    72→	Value string `json:"value" example:"bash script"`
    73→}
    74→
    75→// JobMetaRequest model
    76→type JobMetaRequest struct {
    77→	JobId     *int64          `json:"jobId" validate:"required" example:"123000"` // Cluster Job ID of job
    78→	Cluster   *string         `json:"cluster" example:"fritz"`                    // Cluster of job
    79→	StartTime *int64          `json:"startTime" example:"1649723812"`             // Start Time of job as epoch
    80→	Payload   EditMetaRequest `json:"payload"`                                    // Content to Add to Job Meta_Data
    81→}
    82→
    83→type TagJobAPIRequest []*APITag
    84→
    85→type GetJobAPIRequest []string
    86→
    87→type GetJobAPIResponse struct {
    88→	Meta *schema.Job
    89→	Data []*JobMetricWithName
    90→}
    91→
    92→type GetCompleteJobAPIResponse struct {
    93→	Meta *schema.Job
    94→	Data schema.JobData
    95→}
    96→
    97→type JobMetricWithName struct {
    98→	Metric *schema.JobMetric  `json:"metric"`
    99→	Name   string             `json:"name"`
   100→	Scope  schema.MetricScope `json:"scope"`
   101→}
   102→
   103→// getJobs godoc
   104→// @summary     Lists all jobs
   105→// @tags Job query
   106→// @description Get a list of all jobs. Filters can be applied using query parameters.
   107→// @description Number of results can be limited by page. Results are sorted by descending startTime.
   108→// @produce     json
   109→// @param       state          query    string            false "Job State" Enums(running, completed, failed, cancelled, stopped, timeout)
   110→// @param       cluster        query    string            false "Job Cluster"
   111→// @param       start-time     query    string            false "Syntax: '$from-$to', as unix epoch timestamps in seconds"
   112→// @param       items-per-page query    int               false "Items per page (Default: 25)"
   113→// @param       page           query    int               false "Page Number (Default: 1)"
   114→// @param       with-metadata  query    bool              false "Include metadata (e.g. jobScript) in response"
   115→// @success     200            {object} api.GetJobsAPIResponse  "Job array and page info"
   116→// @failure     400            {object} api.ErrorResponse       "Bad Request"
   117→// @failure     401   		   {object} api.ErrorResponse       "Unauthorized"
   118→// @failure     403            {object} api.ErrorResponse       "Forbidden"
   119→// @failure     500            {object} api.ErrorResponse       "Internal Server Error"
   120→// @security    ApiKeyAuth
   121→// @router      /api/jobs/ [get]
   122→func (api *RestAPI) getJobs(rw http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
   123→	withMetadata := false
   124→	filter := &model.JobFilter{}
   125→	page := &model.PageRequest{ItemsPerPage: 25, Page: 1}
   126→	order := &model.OrderByInput{Field: "startTime", Type: "col", Order: model.SortDirectionEnumDesc}
   127→
   128→	for key, vals := range r.URL.Query() {
   129→		switch key {
   130→		case "project":
   131→			filter.Project = &model.StringInput{Eq: &vals[0]}
   132→		case "state":
   133→			for _, s := range vals {
   134→				state := schema.JobState(s)
   135→				if !state.Valid() {
   136→					handleError(fmt.Errorf("invalid query parameter value: state"),
   137→						http.StatusBadRequest, rw)
   138→					return
   139→				}
   140→				filter.State = append(filter.State, state)
   141→			}
   142→		case "cluster":
   143→			filter.Cluster = &model.StringInput{Eq: &vals[0]}
   144→		case "start-time": // ?startTime=1753707480-1754053139
   145→			st := strings.Split(vals[0], "-")
   146→			if len(st) != 2 {
   147→				handleError(fmt.Errorf("invalid query parameter value: startTime"),
   148→					http.StatusBadRequest, rw)
   149→				return
   150→			}
   151→			from, err := strconv.ParseInt(st[0], 10, 64)
   152→			if err != nil {
   153→				handleError(err, http.StatusBadRequest, rw)
   154→				return
   155→			}
   156→			to, err := strconv.ParseInt(st[1], 10, 64)
   157→			if err != nil {
   158→				handleError(err, http.StatusBadRequest, rw)
   159→				return
   160→			}
   161→			ufrom, uto := time.Unix(from, 0), time.Unix(to, 0)
   162→			filter.StartTime = &config.TimeRange{From: &ufrom, To: &uto}
   163→		case "page":
   164→			x, err := strconv.Atoi(vals[0])
   165→			if err != nil {
   166→				handleError(err, http.StatusBadRequest, rw)
   167→				return
   168→			}
   169→			page.Page = x
   170→		case "items-per-page":
   171→			x, err := strconv.Atoi(vals[0])
   172→			if err != nil {
   173→				handleError(err, http.StatusBadRequest, rw)
   174→				return
   175→			}
   176→			page.ItemsPerPage = x
   177→		case "with-metadata":
   178→			withMetadata = true
   179→		default:
   180→			handleError(fmt.Errorf("invalid query parameter: %s", key),
   181→				http.StatusBadRequest, rw)
   182→			return
   183→		}
   184→	}
   185→
   186→	jobs, err := api.JobRepository.QueryJobs(r.Context(), []*model.JobFilter{filter}, page, order)
   187→	if err != nil {
   188→		handleError(err, http.StatusInternalServerError, rw)
   189→		return
   190→	}
   191→
   192→	results := make([]*schema.Job, 0, len(jobs))
   193→	for _, job := range jobs {
   194→		if withMetadata {
   195→			if _, err = api.JobRepository.FetchMetadata(job); err != nil {
   196→				handleError(err, http.StatusInternalServerError, rw)
   197→				return
   198→			}
   199→		}
   200→

1→// Copyright (C) NHR@FAU, University Erlangen-Nuremberg.
     2→// All rights reserved. This file is part of cc-backend.
     3→// Use of this source code is governed by a MIT-style
     4→// license that can be found in the LICENSE file.
     5→
     6→package api
     7→
     8→import (
     9→	"database/sql"
    10→	"encoding/json"
    11→	"strings"
    12→	"sync"
    13→	"time"
    14→
    15→	"github.com/ClusterCockpit/cc-backend/internal/archiver"
    16→	"github.com/ClusterCockpit/cc-backend/internal/config"
    17→	"github.com/ClusterCockpit/cc-backend/internal/importer"
    18→	"github.com/ClusterCockpit/cc-backend/internal/repository"
    19→	cclog "github.com/ClusterCockpit/cc-lib/v2/ccLogger"
    20→	lp "github.com/ClusterCockpit/cc-lib/v2/ccMessage"
    21→	"github.com/ClusterCockpit/cc-lib/v2/nats"
    22→	"github.com/ClusterCockpit/cc-lib/v2/receivers"
    23→	"github.com/ClusterCockpit/cc-lib/v2/schema"
    24→	influx "github.com/ClusterCockpit/cc-line-protocol/v2/lineprotocol"
    25→)
    26→
    27→// NatsAPI provides NATS subscription-based handlers for Job and Node operations.
    28→// It mirrors the functionality of the REST API but uses NATS messaging with
    29→// InfluxDB line protocol as the message format.
    30→//
    31→// # Message Format
    32→//
    33→// All NATS messages use InfluxDB line protocol format (https://docs.influxdata.com/influxdb/v2.0/reference/syntax/line-protocol/)
    34→// with the following structure:
    35→//
    36→//	measurement,tag1=value1,tag2=value2 field1=value1,field2=value2 timestamp
    37→//
    38→// # Job Events
    39→//
    40→// Job start/stop events use the "job" measurement with a "function" tag to distinguish operations:
    41→//
    42→//	job,function=start_job event="{...JSON payload...}" <timestamp>
    43→//	job,function=stop_job event="{...JSON payload...}" <timestamp>
    44→//
    45→// The JSON payload in the "event" field follows the schema.Job or StopJobAPIRequest structure.
    46→//
    47→// Example job start message:
    48→//
    49→//	job,function=start_job event="{\"jobId\":1001,\"user\":\"testuser\",\"cluster\":\"testcluster\",...}" 1234567890000000000
    50→//
    51→// # Node State Events
    52→//
    53→// Node state updates use the "nodestate" measurement with cluster information:
    54→//
    55→//	nodestate event="{...JSON payload...}" <timestamp>
    56→//
    57→// The JSON payload follows the UpdateNodeStatesRequest structure.
    58→//
    59→// Example node state message:
    60→//
    61→//	nodestate event="{\"cluster\":\"testcluster\",\"nodes\":[{\"hostname\":\"node01\",\"states\":[\"idle\"]}]}" 1234567890000000000
    62→type NatsAPI struct {
    63→	JobRepository *repository.JobRepository
    64→	// RepositoryMutex protects job creation operations from race conditions
    65→	// when checking for duplicate jobs during startJob calls.
    66→	RepositoryMutex sync.Mutex
    67→	// jobSem limits concurrent NATS job event processing to prevent goroutine accumulation
    68→	// when the database is under contention.
    69→	jobSem chan struct{}
    70→	// nodeSem limits concurrent NATS node state processing.
    71→	nodeSem chan struct{}
    72→}
    73→
    74→// NewNatsAPI creates a new NatsAPI instance with default dependencies.
    75→func NewNatsAPI() *NatsAPI {
    76→	return &NatsAPI{
    77→		JobRepository: repository.GetJobRepository(),
    78→		jobSem:        make(chan struct{}, 8),
    79→		nodeSem:       make(chan struct{}, 2),
    80→	}
    81→}
    82→
    83→// StartSubscriptions registers all NATS subscriptions for Job and Node APIs.
    84→// Returns an error if the NATS client is not available or subscription fails.
    85→func (api *NatsAPI) StartSubscriptions() error {
    86→	client := nats.GetClient()
    87→	if client == nil {
    88→		cclog.Warn("NATS client not available, skipping API subscriptions")
    89→		return nil
    90→	}
    91→
    92→	if config.Keys.APISubjects != nil {
    93→
    94→		s := config.Keys.APISubjects
    95→
    96→		if err := client.Subscribe(s.SubjectJobEvent, api.handleJobEvent); err != nil {
    97→			return err
    98→		}
    99→
   100→		if err := client.Subscribe(s.SubjectNodeState, api.handleNodeState); err != nil {
   101→			return err
   102→		}
   103→
   104→		cclog.Info("NATS API subscriptions started")
   105→	}
   106→	return nil
   107→}
   108→
   109→// processJobEvent routes job event messages to the appropriate handler based on the "function" tag.
   110→// Validates that required tags and fields are present before processing.
   111→func (api *NatsAPI) processJobEvent(msg lp.CCMessage) {
   112→	function, ok := msg.GetTag("function")
   113→	if !ok {
   114→		cclog.Errorf("Job event is missing required tag 'function': measurement=%s", msg.Name())
   115→		return
   116→	}
   117→
   118→	switch function {
   119→	case "start_job":
   120→		v, ok := msg.GetEventValue()
   121→		if !ok {
   122→			cclog.Errorf("Job start event is missing event field with JSON payload")
   123→			return
   124→		}
   125→		api.handleStartJob(v)
   126→
   127→	case "stop_job":
   128→		v, ok := msg.GetEventValue()
   129→		if !ok {
   130→			cclog.Errorf("Job stop event is missing event field with JSON payload")
   131→			return
   132→		}
   133→		api.handleStopJob(v)
   134→
   135→	default:
   136→		cclog.Warnf("Unknown job event function '%s', expected 'start_job' or 'stop_job'", function)
   137→	}
   138→}
   139→
   140→// handleJobEvent processes job-related messages received via NATS using InfluxDB line protocol.
   141→// The message must be in line protocol format with measurement="job" and include:
   142→//   - tag "function" with value "start_job" or "stop_job"
   143→//   - field "event" containing JSON payload (schema.Job or StopJobAPIRequest)
   144→//
   145→// Example: job,function=start_job event="{\"jobId\":1001,...}" 1234567890000000000
   146→func (api *NatsAPI) handleJobEvent(subject string, data []byte) {
   147→	api.jobSem <- struct{}{}
   148→	defer func() { <-api.jobSem }()
   149→
   150→	if len(data) == 0 {
   151→		cclog.Warnf("NATS %s: received empty message", subject)
   152→		return
   153→	}
   154→
   155→	d := influx.NewDecoderWithBytes(data)
   156→
   157→	for d.Next() {
   158→		m, err := receivers.DecodeInfluxMessage(d)
   159→		if err != nil {
   160→			cclog.Errorf("NATS %s: failed to decode InfluxDB line protocol message: %v", subject, err)
   161→			return
   162→		}
   163→
   164→		if !m.IsEvent() {
   165→			cclog.Debugf("NATS %s: received non-event message, skipping", subject)
   166→			continue
   167→		}
   168→
   169→		if m.Name() == "job" {
   170→			api.processJobEvent(m)
   171→		} else {
   172→			cclog.Debugf("NATS %s: unexpected measurement name '%s', expected 'job'", subject, m.Name())
   173→		}
   174→	}
   175→}
   176→
   177→// handleStartJob processes job start messages received via NATS.
   178→// The payload parameter contains JSON following the schema.Job structure.
   179→// Jobs are validated, checked for duplicates, and inserted into the database.
   180→func (api *NatsAPI) handleStartJob(payload string) {
   181→	if payload == "" {
   182→		cclog.Error("NATS start job: payload is empty")
   183→		return
   184→	}
   185→	req := schema.Job{
   186→		Shared:           "none",
   187→		MonitoringStatus: schema.MonitoringStatusRunningOrArchiving,
   188→	}
   189→
   190→	dec := json.NewDecoder(strings.NewReader(payload))
   191→	dec.DisallowUnknownFields()
   192→	if err := dec.Decode(&req); err != nil {
   193→		cclog.Errorf("NATS start job: parsing request failed: %v", err)
   194→		return
   195→	}
   196→
   197→	cclog.Debugf("NATS start job: %s", req.GoString())
   198→	req.State = schema.JobStateRunning
   199→
   200→	if err := importer.SanityChecks(&req); err != nil {
   201→		cclog.Errorf("NATS start job: sanity check failed: %v", err)
   202→		return
   203→	}
   204→
   205→	var unlockOnce sync.Once
   206→	api.RepositoryMutex.Lock()
   207→	defer unlockOnce.Do(api.RepositoryMutex.Unlock)
   208→
   209→	jobs, err := api.JobRepository.FindAll(&req.JobID, &req.Cluster, nil)
   210→	if err != nil && err != sql.ErrNoRows {
   211→		cclog.Errorf("NATS start job: checking for duplicate failed: %v", err)
   212→		return
   213→	}
   214→	if err == nil {
   215→		for _, job := range jobs {
   216→			if (req.StartTime - job.StartTime) < secondsPerDay {
   217→				cclog.Errorf("NATS start job: job with jobId %d, cluster %s already exists (dbid: %d)",
   218→					req.JobID, req.Cluster, job.ID)
   219→				return
   220→			}
   221→		}
   222→	}
   223→
   224→	// When tags are present, insert directly into the job table so that the
   225→	// returned ID can be used with AddTagOrCreate (which queries the job table).
   226→	var id int64
   227→	if len(req.Tags) > 0 {
   228→		id, err = api.JobRepository.StartDirect(&req)
   229→	} else {
   230→		id, err = api.JobRepository.Start(&req)
   231→	}
   232→	if err != nil {
   233→		cclog.Errorf("NATS start job: insert into database failed: %v", err)
   234→		return
   235→	}
   236→	unlockOnce.Do(api.RepositoryMutex.Unlock)
   237→
   238→	for _, tag := range req.Tags {
   239→		if _, err := api.JobRepository.AddTagOrCreate(nil, id, tag.Type, tag.Name, tag.Scope); err != nil {
   240→			cclog.Errorf("NATS start job: adding tag to new job %d failed: %v", id, err)
   241→			return
   242→		}
   243→	}
   244→
   245→	cclog.Infof("NATS: new job (id: %d): cluster=%s, jobId=%d, user=%s, startTime=%d",
   246→		id, req.Cluster, req.JobID, req.User, req.StartTime)
   247→}
   248→
   249→// handleStopJob processes job stop messages received via NATS.
   250→// The payload parameter contains JSON following the StopJobAPIRequest structure.
   251→// The job is marked as stopped in the database and archiving is triggered if monitoring is enabled.
   252→func (api *NatsAPI) handleStopJob(payload string) {
   253→	if payload == "" {
   254→		cclog.Error("NATS stop job: payload is empty")
   255→		return
   256→	}
   257→	var req StopJobAPIRequest
   258→
   259→	dec := json.NewDecoder(strings.NewReader(payload))
   260→	dec.DisallowUnknownFields()
   261→	if err := dec.Decode(&req); err != nil {
   262→		cclog.Errorf("NATS job stop: parsing request failed: %v", err)
   263→		return
   264→	}
   265→
   266→	if req.JobID == nil {
   267→		cclog.Errorf("NATS job stop: the field 'jobId' is required")
   268→		return
   269→	}
   270→
   271→	isCached := false
   272→	job, err := api.JobRepository.FindCached(req.JobID, req.Cluster, req.StartTime)
   273→	if err != nil {
   274→		// Not in cache, try main job table
   275→		job, err = api.JobRepository.Find(req.JobID, req.Cluster, req.StartTime)
   276→		if err != nil {
   277→			cclog.Errorf("NATS job stop: finding job failed: %v", err)
   278→			return
   279→		}
   280→	} else {
   281→		isCached = true
   282→	}
   283→
   284→	if job.State != schema.JobStateRunning {
   285→		cclog.Errorf("NATS job stop: jobId %d (id %d) on %s: job has already been stopped (state is: %s)",
   286→			job.JobID, job.ID, job.Cluster, job.State)
   287→		return
   288→	}
   289→
   290→	if job.StartTime > req.StopTime {
   291→		cclog.Errorf("NATS job stop: jobId %d (id %d) on %s: stopTime %d must be >= startTime %d",
   292→			job.JobID, job.ID, job.Cluster, req.StopTime, job.StartTime)
   293→		return
   294→	}
   295→
   296→	if req.State != "" && !req.State.Valid() {
   297→		cclog.Errorf("NATS job stop: jobId %d (id %d) on %s: invalid job state: %#v",
   298→			job.JobID, job.ID, job.Cluster, req.State)
   299→		return
   300→	} else if req.State == "" {

    11→	"github.com/ClusterCockpit/cc-backend/internal/repository"

    15→
    16→func RegisterCommitJobService() {
    17→	var frequency string
    18→	if Keys.CommitJobWorker != "" {
    19→		frequency = Keys.CommitJobWorker
    20→	} else {
    21→		frequency = "2m"
    22→	}
    23→
    24→	d, err := parseDuration(frequency)
    25→	if err != nil {
    26→		cclog.Errorf("RegisterCommitJobService: %v", err)
    27→		return
    28→	}
    29→
    30→	cclog.Infof("register commitJob service with %s interval", frequency)
    31→
    32→	s.NewJob(gocron.DurationJob(d),
    33→		gocron.NewTask(
    34→			func() {
    35→				start := time.Now()
    36→				cclog.Debugf("jobcache sync started at %s\n", start.Format(time.RFC3339))
    37→				jobs, _ := jobRepo.SyncJobs()
    38→				repository.CallJobStartHooks(jobs)
    39→				cclog.Debugf("jobcache sync and job callbacks are done and took %s\n", time.Since(start))
    40→			}))
    41→}

    16→	"github.com/ClusterCockpit/cc-backend/internal/repository"
    17→	"github.com/ClusterCockpit/cc-backend/pkg/archive"
    18→	"github.com/ClusterCockpit/cc-backend/pkg/metricstore"

    22→
    23→type UpdateNodeStatesRequest struct {
    24→	Nodes   []schema.NodePayload `json:"nodes"`
    25→	Cluster string               `json:"cluster" example:"fritz"`
    26→}
    27→
    28→// metricListToNames converts a map of metric configurations to a list of metric names
    29→func metricListToNames(metricList map[string]*schema.Metric) []string {
    30→	names := make([]string, 0, len(metricList))
    31→	for name := range metricList {
    32→		names = append(names, name)
    33→	}
    34→	return names
    35→}
    36→
    37→// this routine assumes that only one of them exists per node
    38→func determineState(states []string) schema.SchedulerState {
    39→	for _, state := range states {
    40→		switch strings.ToLower(state) {
    41→		case "allocated":
    42→			return schema.NodeStateAllocated
    43→		case "reserved":
    44→			return schema.NodeStateReserved
    45→		case "idle":
    46→			return schema.NodeStateIdle
    47→		case "down":
    48→			return schema.NodeStateDown
    49→		case "mixed":
    50→			return schema.NodeStateMixed
    51→		}
    52→	}
    53→
    54→	return schema.NodeStateUnknown
    55→}
    56→
    57→// updateNodeStates godoc
    58→// @summary     Deliver updated Slurm node states
    59→// @tags Nodestates
    60→// @description Returns a JSON-encoded list of users.
    61→// @description Required query-parameter defines if all users or only users with additional special roles are returned.
    62→// @produce     json
    63→// @param       request body UpdateNodeStatesRequest true "Request body containing nodes and their states"
    64→// @success     200     {object} api.DefaultAPIResponse "Success message"
    65→// @failure     400     {object} api.ErrorResponse      "Bad Request"
    66→// @failure     401     {object} api.ErrorResponse      "Unauthorized"
    67→// @failure     403     {object} api.ErrorResponse      "Forbidden"
    68→// @failure     500     {object} api.ErrorResponse      "Internal Server Error"
    69→// @security    ApiKeyAuth
    70→// @router      /api/nodestats/ [post]
    71→func (api *RestAPI) updateNodeStates(rw http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
    72→	// Parse request body
    73→	req := UpdateNodeStatesRequest{}
    74→	if err := decode(r.Body, &req); err != nil {
    75→		handleError(fmt.Errorf("parsing request body failed: %w", err),
    76→			http.StatusBadRequest, rw)
    77→		return
    78→	}
    79→	requestReceived := time.Now().Unix()
    80→	repo := repository.GetNodeRepository()
    81→
    82→	m := make(map[string][]string)
    83→	metricNames := make(map[string][]string)
    84→	healthResults := make(map[string]metricstore.HealthCheckResult)
    85→
    86→	startMs := time.Now()
    87→
    88→	// Step 1: Build nodeList and metricList per subcluster
    89→	for _, node := range req.Nodes {
    90→		if sc, err := archive.GetSubClusterByNode(req.Cluster, node.Hostname); err == nil {
    91→			m[sc] = append(m[sc], node.Hostname)
    92→		}
    93→	}
    94→
    95→	for sc := range m {
    96→		if sc != "" {
    97→			metricList := archive.GetMetricConfigSubCluster(req.Cluster, sc)
    98→			metricNames[sc] = metricListToNames(metricList)
    99→		}
   100→	}
   101→
   102→	// Step 2: Determine which metric store to query and perform health check
   103→	healthRepo, err := metricdispatch.GetHealthCheckRepo(req.Cluster)
   104→	if err != nil {
   105→		cclog.Warnf("updateNodeStates: no metric store for cluster %s, skipping health check: %v", req.Cluster, err)
   106→	} else {
   107→		for sc, nl := range m {
   108→			if sc != "" {
   109→				if results, err := healthRepo.HealthCheck(req.Cluster, nl, metricNames[sc]); err == nil {
   110→					maps.Copy(healthResults, results)
   111→				}
   112→			}
   113→		}
   114→	}
   115→
   116→	cclog.Debugf("Timer updateNodeStates, MemStore HealthCheck: %s", time.Since(startMs))
   117→	startDB := time.Now()
   118→
   119→	updates := make([]repository.NodeStateUpdate, 0, len(req.Nodes))
   120→	for _, node := range req.Nodes {
   121→		state := determineState(node.States)
   122→		healthState := schema.MonitoringStateFailed
   123→		var healthMetrics string
   124→		if result, ok := healthResults[node.Hostname]; ok {
   125→			healthState = result.State
   126→			healthMetrics = result.HealthMetrics
   127→		}
   128→		nodeState := schema.NodeStateDB{
   129→			TimeStamp:       requestReceived,
   130→			NodeState:       state,
   131→			CpusAllocated:   node.CpusAllocated,
   132→			MemoryAllocated: node.MemoryAllocated,
   133→			GpusAllocated:   node.GpusAllocated,
   134→			HealthState:     healthState,
   135→			HealthMetrics:   healthMetrics,
   136→			JobsRunning:     node.JobsRunning,
   137→		}
   138→		updates = append(updates, repository.NodeStateUpdate{
   139→			Hostname:  node.Hostname,
   140→			Cluster:   req.Cluster,
   141→			NodeState: &nodeState,
   142→		})
   143→	}
   144→
   145→	if err := repo.BatchUpdateNodeStates(updates); err != nil {
   146→		cclog.Errorf("updateNodeStates: batch update for cluster %s failed: %v", req.Cluster, err)
   147→	}
   148→
   149→	cclog.Debugf("Timer updateNodeStates, SQLite Inserts: %s", time.Since(startDB))
   150→}

    27→// getUsers godoc
    28→// @summary     Returns a list of users
    29→// @tags User
    30→// @description Returns a JSON-encoded list of users.
    31→// @description Required query-parameter defines if all users or only users with additional special roles are returned.
    32→// @produce     json
    33→// @param       not-just-user query bool true "If returned list should contain all users or only users with additional special roles"
    34→// @success     200     {array} api.APIReturnedUser "List of users returned successfully"
    35→// @failure     400     {string} string             "Bad Request"
    36→// @failure     401     {string} string             "Unauthorized"
    37→// @failure     403     {string} string             "Forbidden"
    38→// @failure     500     {string} string             "Internal Server Error"
    39→// @security    ApiKeyAuth
    40→// @router      /api/users/ [get]
    41→func (api *RestAPI) getUsers(rw http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
    42→	// SecuredCheck() only worked with TokenAuth: Removed
    43→
    44→	if user := repository.GetUserFromContext(r.Context()); !user.HasRole(schema.RoleAdmin) {
    45→		handleError(fmt.Errorf("only admins are allowed to fetch a list of users"), http.StatusForbidden, rw)
    46→		return
    47→	}
    48→
    49→	users, err := repository.GetUserRepository().ListUsers(r.URL.Query().Get("not-just-user") == "true")
    50→	if err != nil {
    51→		handleError(fmt.Errorf("listing users failed: %w", err), http.StatusInternalServerError, rw)
    52→		return
    53→	}
    54→
    55→	rw.Header().Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
    56→	if err := json.NewEncoder(rw).Encode(users); err != nil {
    57→		cclog.Errorf("Failed to encode users response: %v", err)
    58→	}
    59→}

    61→// updateUser godoc
    62→// @summary     Update user roles and projects
    63→// @tags User
    64→// @description Allows admins to add/remove roles and projects for a user
    65→// @produce     plain
    66→// @param       id          path   string  true  "Username"
    67→// @param       add-role    formData string false "Role to add"
    68→// @param       remove-role formData string false "Role to remove"
    69→// @param       add-project formData string false "Project to add"
    70→// @param       remove-project formData string false "Project to remove"
    71→// @success     200     {string} string "Success message"
    72→// @failure     403     {object} api.ErrorResponse "Forbidden"
    73→// @failure     422     {object} api.ErrorResponse "Unprocessable Entity"
    74→// @security    ApiKeyAuth
    75→// @router      /api/user/{id} [post]
    76→func (api *RestAPI) updateUser(rw http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
    77→	// SecuredCheck() only worked with TokenAuth: Removed
    78→
    79→	if user := repository.GetUserFromContext(r.Context()); !user.HasRole(schema.RoleAdmin) {
    80→		handleError(fmt.Errorf("only admins are allowed to update a user"), http.StatusForbidden, rw)
    81→		return
    82→	}
    83→
    84→	// Get Values
    85→	newrole := r.FormValue("add-role")
    86→	delrole := r.FormValue("remove-role")
    87→	newproj := r.FormValue("add-project")
    88→	delproj := r.FormValue("remove-project")
    89→
    90→	rw.Header().Set("Content-Type", "application/json")
    91→
    92→	// Handle role updates
    93→	if newrole != "" {
    94→		if err := repository.GetUserRepository().AddRole(r.Context(), chi.URLParam(r, "id"), newrole); err != nil {
    95→			handleError(fmt.Errorf("adding role failed: %w", err), http.StatusUnprocessableEntity, rw)
    96→			return
    97→		}
    98→		if err := json.NewEncoder(rw).Encode(DefaultAPIResponse{Message: "Add Role Success"}); err != nil {
    99→			cclog.Errorf("Failed to encode response: %v", err)
   100→		}
   101→	} else if delrole != "" {
   102→		if err := repository.GetUserRepository().RemoveRole(r.Context(), chi.URLParam(r, "id"), delrole); err != nil {
   103→			handleError(fmt.Errorf("removing role failed: %w", err), http.StatusUnprocessableEntity, rw)
   104→			return
   105→		}
   106→		if err := json.NewEncoder(rw).Encode(DefaultAPIResponse{Message: "Remove Role Success"}); err != nil {
   107→			cclog.Errorf("Failed to encode response: %v", err)
   108→		}
   109→	} else if newproj != "" {
   110→		if err := repository.GetUserRepository().AddProject(r.Context(), chi.URLParam(r, "id"), newproj); err != nil {
   111→			handleError(fmt.Errorf("adding project failed: %w", err), http.StatusUnprocessableEntity, rw)
   112→			return
   113→		}
   114→		if err := json.NewEncoder(rw).Encode(DefaultAPIResponse{Message: "Add Project Success"}); err != nil {
   115→			cclog.Errorf("Failed to encode response: %v", err)
   116→		}
   117→	} else if delproj != "" {
   118→		if err := repository.GetUserRepository().RemoveProject(r.Context(), chi.URLParam(r, "id"), delproj); err != nil {
   119→			handleError(fmt.Errorf("removing project failed: %w", err), http.StatusUnprocessableEntity, rw)
   120→			return
   121→		}
   122→		if err := json.NewEncoder(rw).Encode(DefaultAPIResponse{Message: "Remove Project Success"}); err != nil {
   123→			cclog.Errorf("Failed to encode response: %v", err)
   124→		}
   125→	} else {
   126→		handleError(fmt.Errorf("no operation specified: must provide add-role, remove-role, add-project, or remove-project"), http.StatusBadRequest, rw)
   127→	}
   128→}

   130→// createUser godoc
   131→// @summary     Create a new user
   132→// @tags User
   133→// @description Creates a new user with specified credentials and role
   134→// @produce     plain
   135→// @param       username formData string true  "Username"
   136→// @param       password formData string false "Password (not required for API users)"
   137→// @param       role     formData string true  "User role"
   138→// @param       name     formData string false "Full name"
   139→// @param       email    formData string false "Email address"
   140→// @param       project  formData string false "Project (required for managers)"
   141→// @success     200     {string} string "Success message"
   142→// @failure     400     {object} api.ErrorResponse "Bad Request"
   143→// @failure     403     {object} api.ErrorResponse "Forbidden"
   144→// @failure     422     {object} api.ErrorResponse "Unprocessable Entity"
   145→// @security    ApiKeyAuth
   146→// @router      /api/users/ [post]
   147→func (api *RestAPI) createUser(rw http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
   148→	// SecuredCheck() only worked with TokenAuth: Removed
   149→
   150→	rw.Header().Set("Content-Type", "text/plain")
   151→	me := repository.GetUserFromContext(r.Context())
   152→	if !me.HasRole(schema.RoleAdmin) {
   153→		handleError(fmt.Errorf("only admins are allowed to create new users"), http.StatusForbidden, rw)
   154→		return
   155→	}
   156→
   157→	username, password, role, name, email, project := r.FormValue("username"),
   158→		r.FormValue("password"), r.FormValue("role"), r.FormValue("name"),
   159→		r.FormValue("email"), r.FormValue("project")
   160→
   161→	// Validate username length
   162→	if len(username) == 0 || len(username) > 100 {
   163→		handleError(fmt.Errorf("username must be between 1 and 100 characters"), http.StatusBadRequest, rw)
   164→		return
   165→	}
   166→
   167→	if len(password) == 0 && role != schema.GetRoleString(schema.RoleAPI) {
   168→		handleError(fmt.Errorf("only API users are allowed to have a blank password (login will be impossible)"), http.StatusBadRequest, rw)
   169→		return
   170→	}
   171→
   172→	if len(project) != 0 && role != schema.GetRoleString(schema.RoleManager) {
   173→		handleError(fmt.Errorf("only managers require a project (can be changed later)"), http.StatusBadRequest, rw)
   174→		return
   175→	} else if len(project) == 0 && role == schema.GetRoleString(schema.RoleManager) {
   176→		handleError(fmt.Errorf("managers require a project to manage (can be changed later)"), http.StatusBadRequest, rw)
   177→		return
   178→	}
   179→
   180→	if err := repository.GetUserRepository().AddUser(&schema.User{
   181→		Username: username,
   182→		Name:     name,
   183→		Password: password,
   184→		Email:    email,
   185→		Projects: []string{project},
   186→		Roles:    []string{role},
   187→	}); err != nil {
   188→		handleError(fmt.Errorf("adding user failed: %w", err), http.StatusUnprocessableEntity, rw)
   189→		return
   190→	}
   191→
   192→	fmt.Fprintf(rw, "User %v successfully created!\n", username)
   193→}

1→// Copyright (C) NHR@FAU, University Erlangen-Nuremberg.

     6→package taskmanager
     7→
     8→import (
     9→	"context"
    10→	"math"
    11→	"time"
    12→
    13→	"github.com/ClusterCockpit/cc-backend/internal/metricdispatch"
    14→	"github.com/ClusterCockpit/cc-backend/pkg/archive"
    15→	cclog "github.com/ClusterCockpit/cc-lib/v2/ccLogger"
    16→	"github.com/ClusterCockpit/cc-lib/v2/schema"
    17→	sq "github.com/Masterminds/squirrel"
    18→	"github.com/go-co-op/gocron/v2"
    19→)
    20→
    21→func RegisterFootprintWorker() {
    22→	var frequency string
    23→	if Keys.FootprintWorker != "" {
    24→		frequency = Keys.FootprintWorker
    25→	} else {
    26→		frequency = "10m"
    27→	}
    28→
    29→	d, err := parseDuration(frequency)
    30→	if err != nil {
    31→		cclog.Errorf("RegisterFootprintWorker: %v", err)
    32→		return
    33→	}
    34→
    35→	cclog.Infof("Register Footprint Update service with %s interval", frequency)
    36→
    37→	s.NewJob(gocron.DurationJob(d),
    38→		gocron.NewTask(
    39→			func() {
    40→				s := time.Now()
    41→				c := 0
    42→				ce := 0
    43→				cl := 0
    44→				cclog.Infof("Update Footprints started at %s", s.Format(time.RFC3339))
    45→
    46→				for _, cluster := range archive.Clusters {
    47→					sCluster := time.Now()
    48→					jobs, err := jobRepo.FindRunningJobs(cluster.Name)
    49→					if err != nil {
    50→						continue
    51→					}
    52→					// NOTE: Additional SubCluster Loop Could Allow For Limited List Of Footprint-Metrics Only.
    53→					//       - Chunk-Size Would Then Be 'SubCluster' (Running Jobs, Transactions) as Lists Can Change Within SCs
    54→					//       - Would Require Review of 'updateFootprint' Usage (Logic Could Possibly Be Included Here Completely)
    55→					allMetrics := make([]string, 0)
    56→					metricConfigs := archive.GetCluster(cluster.Name).MetricConfig
    57→					for _, mc := range metricConfigs {
    58→						allMetrics = append(allMetrics, mc.Name)
    59→					}
    60→
    61→					pendingStatements := []sq.UpdateBuilder{}
    62→
    63→					for _, job := range jobs {
    64→						cclog.Debugf("Prepare job %d", job.JobID)
    65→						cl++
    66→
    67→						sJob := time.Now()
    68→
    69→						ms, err := metricdispatch.GetMetricDataRepo(job.Cluster, job.SubCluster)
    70→						if err != nil {
    71→							cclog.Errorf("failed to access metricDataRepo for cluster %s-%s: %s",
    72→								job.Cluster, job.SubCluster, err.Error())
    73→							continue
    74→						}
    75→
    76→						jobStats, err := ms.LoadStats(job, allMetrics, context.Background())
    77→						if err != nil {
    78→							cclog.Errorf("error wile loading job data stats for footprint update: %v", err)
    79→							ce++
    80→							continue
    81→						}
    82→
    83→						job.Statistics = make(map[string]schema.JobStatistics)
    84→
    85→						for _, metric := range allMetrics {
    86→							avg, min, max := 0.0, 0.0, 0.0
    87→							data, ok := jobStats[metric] // JobStats[Metric1:[Hostname1:[Stats], Hostname2:[Stats], ...], Metric2[...] ...]
    88→							if ok {
    89→								for _, res := range job.Resources {
    90→									hostStats, ok := data[res.Hostname]
    91→									if ok {
    92→										avg += hostStats.Avg
    93→										min = math.Min(min, hostStats.Min)
    94→										max = math.Max(max, hostStats.Max)
    95→									}
    96→
    97→								}
    98→							}
    99→
   100→							// Add values rounded to 2 digits: repo.LoadStats may return unrounded
   101→							job.Statistics[metric] = schema.JobStatistics{
   102→								Unit: schema.Unit{
   103→									Prefix: archive.GetMetricConfig(job.Cluster, metric).Unit.Prefix,
   104→									Base:   archive.GetMetricConfig(job.Cluster, metric).Unit.Base,
   105→								},
   106→								Avg: (math.Round((avg/float64(job.NumNodes))*100) / 100),
   107→								Min: (math.Round(min*100) / 100),
   108→								Max: (math.Round(max*100) / 100),
   109→							}
   110→						}
   111→
   112→						// Build Statement per Job, Add to Pending Array
   113→						stmt := sq.Update("job")
   114→						stmt, err = jobRepo.UpdateFootprint(stmt, job)
   115→						if err != nil {
   116→							cclog.Errorf("update job (dbid: %d) statement build failed at footprint step: %s", *job.ID, err.Error())
   117→							ce++
   118→							continue
   119→						}
   120→						stmt = stmt.Where("job.id = ?", job.ID)
   121→
   122→						pendingStatements = append(pendingStatements, stmt)
   123→						cclog.Debugf("Job %d took %s", job.JobID, time.Since(sJob))
   124→					}
   125→
   126→					t, err := jobRepo.TransactionInit()
   127→					if err != nil {
   128→						cclog.Errorf("failed TransactionInit %v", err)
   129→						cclog.Errorf("skipped %d transactions for cluster %s", len(pendingStatements), cluster.Name)
   130→						ce += len(pendingStatements)
   131→					} else {
   132→						for _, ps := range pendingStatements {
   133→							query, args, err := ps.ToSql()
   134→							if err != nil {
   135→								cclog.Errorf("failed in ToSQL conversion: %v", err)
   136→								ce++
   137→							} else {
   138→								// args...: Footprint-JSON, Energyfootprint-JSON, TotalEnergy, JobID
   139→								jobRepo.TransactionAdd(t, query, args...)
   140→								c++
   141→							}
   142→						}
   143→						jobRepo.TransactionEnd(t)
   144→					}
   145→					cclog.Debugf("Finish Cluster %s, took %s\n", cluster.Name, time.Since(sCluster))
   146→				}
   147→				cclog.Infof("Updating %d (of %d; Skipped %d) Footprints is done and took %s", c, cl, ce, time.Since(s))
   148→			}))
   149→}
   150→

     6→package taskmanager
     7→
     8→import (
     9→	"runtime"
    10→
    11→	"github.com/ClusterCockpit/cc-backend/internal/config"
    12→	cclog "github.com/ClusterCockpit/cc-lib/v2/ccLogger"
    13→	"github.com/go-co-op/gocron/v2"
    14→)
    15→
    16→func RegisterStopJobsExceedTime() {
    17→	cclog.Info("Register undead jobs service")
    18→
    19→	s.NewJob(gocron.DailyJob(1, gocron.NewAtTimes(gocron.NewAtTime(3, 0, 0))),
    20→		gocron.NewTask(
    21→			func() {
    22→				err := jobRepo.StopJobsExceedingWalltimeBy(config.Keys.StopJobsExceedingWalltime)
    23→				if err != nil {
    24→					cclog.Warnf("Error while looking for jobs exceeding their walltime: %s", err.Error())
    25→				}
    26→				runtime.GC()
    27→			}))
    28→}

1→// Copyright (C) NHR@FAU, University Erlangen-Nuremberg.
     2→// All rights reserved. This file is part of cc-backend.
     3→// Use of this source code is governed by a MIT-style
     4→// license that can be found in the LICENSE file.
     5→
     6→// Package repository provides data access and persistence layer for ClusterCockpit.
     7→//
     8→// This file implements tag management functionality for job categorization and classification.
     9→// Tags support both manual assignment (via REST/GraphQL APIs) and automatic detection
    10→// (via tagger plugins). The implementation includes role-based access control through
    11→// tag scopes and maintains bidirectional consistency between the SQL database and
    12→// the file-based job archive.
    13→//
    14→// Database Schema:
    15→//
    16→//	CREATE TABLE tag (
    17→//	    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    18→//	    tag_type VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    19→//	    tag_name VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL,
    20→//	    tag_scope VARCHAR(255) NOT NULL DEFAULT "global",
    21→//	    CONSTRAINT tag_unique UNIQUE (tag_type, tag_name, tag_scope)
    22→//	);
    23→//
    24→//	CREATE TABLE jobtag (
    25→//	    job_id INTEGER,
    26→//	    tag_id INTEGER,
    27→//	    PRIMARY KEY (job_id, tag_id),
    28→//	    FOREIGN KEY (job_id) REFERENCES job(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    29→//	    FOREIGN KEY (tag_id) REFERENCES tag(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
    30→//	);
    31→//
    32→// The jobtag junction table enables many-to-many relationships between jobs and tags.
    33→// CASCADE deletion ensures referential integrity when jobs or tags are removed.
    34→package repository
    35→
    36→import (
    37→	"errors"
    38→	"fmt"
    39→	"strings"
    40→
    41→	"github.com/ClusterCockpit/cc-backend/pkg/archive"
    42→	cclog "github.com/ClusterCockpit/cc-lib/v2/ccLogger"
    43→	"github.com/ClusterCockpit/cc-lib/v2/schema"
    44→	sq "github.com/Masterminds/squirrel"
    45→)
    46→
    47→// Tag Scope Rules:
    48→//
    49→// Tags in ClusterCockpit have three visibility scopes that control who can see and use them:
    50→//
    51→//  1. "global" - Visible to all users, can be used by anyone
    52→//     Example: System-generated tags like "energy-efficient", "failed", "short"
    53→//
    54→//  2. "private" - Only visible to the creating user
    55→//     Example: Personal notes like "needs-review", "interesting-case"
    56→//
    57→//  3. "admin" - Only visible to users with admin or support roles
    58→//     Example: Internal notes like "hardware-issue", "billing-problem"
    59→//
    60→// Authorization Rules:
    61→//   - Regular users can only create/see "global" and their own "private" tags
    62→//   - Admin/Support can create/see all scopes including "admin" tags
    63→//   - Users can only add tags to jobs they have permission to view
    64→//   - Tag scope is enforced at query time in GetTags() and CountTags()
    65→
    66→// AddTag adds the tag with id `tagId` to the job with the database id `jobId`.
    67→// Requires user authentication for security checks.
    68→//
    69→// The user must have permission to view the job. Tag visibility is determined by scope:
    70→//   - "global" tags: visible to all users
    71→//   - "private" tags: only visible to the tag creator
    72→//   - "admin" tags: only visible to admin/support users
    73→func (r *JobRepository) AddTag(user *schema.User, job int64, tag int64) ([]*schema.Tag, error) {
    74→	j, err := r.FindByIDWithUser(user, job)
    75→	if err != nil {
    76→		cclog.Warnf("Error finding job %d for user %s: %v", job, user.Username, err)
    77→		return nil, err
    78→	}
    79→
    80→	return r.addJobTag(job, tag, j, func() ([]*schema.Tag, error) {
    81→		return r.GetTags(user, &job)
    82→	})
    83→}
    84→
    85→// AddTagDirect adds a tag without user security checks.
    86→// Use only for internal/admin operations.
    87→func (r *JobRepository) AddTagDirect(job int64, tag int64) ([]*schema.Tag, error) {
    88→	j, err := r.FindByIDDirect(job)
    89→	if err != nil {
    90→		cclog.Warnf("Error finding job %d: %v", job, err)
    91→		return nil, err
    92→	}
    93→
    94→	return r.addJobTag(job, tag, j, func() ([]*schema.Tag, error) {
    95→		return r.GetTagsDirect(&job)
    96→	})
    97→}
    98→
    99→// RemoveTag removes the tag with the database id `tag` from the job with the database id `job`.
   100→// Requires user authentication for security checks. Used by GraphQL API.
   101→func (r *JobRepository) RemoveTag(user *schema.User, job, tag int64) ([]*schema.Tag, error) {
   102→	j, err := r.FindByIDWithUser(user, job)
   103→	if err != nil {
   104→		cclog.Warnf("Error while finding job %d for user %s during tag removal: %v", job, user.Username, err)
   105→		return nil, err
   106→	}
   107→
   108→	q := sq.Delete("jobtag").Where("jobtag.job_id = ?", job).Where("jobtag.tag_id = ?", tag)
   109→
   110→	if _, err := q.RunWith(r.stmtCache).Exec(); err != nil {
   111→		s, _, _ := q.ToSql()
   112→		cclog.Errorf("Error removing tag with %s: %v", s, err)
   113→		return nil, err
   114→	}
   115→
   116→	tags, err := r.GetTags(user, &job)
   117→	if err != nil {
   118→		cclog.Warn("Error while getting tags for job")
   119→		return nil, err
   120→	}
   121→
   122→	archiveTags, err := r.getArchiveTags(&job)
   123→	if err != nil {
   124→		cclog.Warnf("Error while getting archive tags for job %d in RemoveTag: %v", job, err)
   125→		return nil, err
   126→	}
   127→
   128→	return tags, archive.UpdateTags(j, archiveTags)
   129→}
   130→
   131→// RemoveJobTagByRequest removes a tag from the job with the database id `job` by tag type, name, and scope.
   132→// Requires user authentication for security checks. Used by REST API.
   133→func (r *JobRepository) RemoveJobTagByRequest(user *schema.User, job int64, tagType string, tagName string, tagScope string) ([]*schema.Tag, error) {
   134→	// Get Tag ID to delete
   135→	tagID, exists := r.TagID(tagType, tagName, tagScope)
   136→	if !exists {
   137→		cclog.Warnf("Tag does not exist (name, type, scope): %s, %s, %s", tagName, tagType, tagScope)
   138→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("tag does not exist (name, type, scope): %s, %s, %s", tagName, tagType, tagScope)
   139→	}
   140→
   141→	// Get Job
   142→	j, err := r.FindByIDWithUser(user, job)
   143→	if err != nil {
   144→		cclog.Warnf("Error while finding job %d for user %s during tag removal by request: %v", job, user.Username, err)
   145→		return nil, err
   146→	}
   147→
   148→	// Handle Delete
   149→	q := sq.Delete("jobtag").Where("jobtag.job_id = ?", job).Where("jobtag.tag_id = ?", tagID)
   150→
   151→	if _, err := q.RunWith(r.stmtCache).Exec(); err != nil {
   152→		s, _, _ := q.ToSql()
   153→		cclog.Errorf("Error removing tag from table 'jobTag' with %s: %v", s, err)
   154→		return nil, err
   155→	}
   156→
   157→	tags, err := r.GetTags(user, &job)
   158→	if err != nil {
   159→		cclog.Warnf("Error while getting tags for job %d in RemoveJobTagByRequest: %v", job, err)
   160→		return nil, err
   161→	}
   162→
   163→	archiveTags, err := r.getArchiveTags(&job)
   164→	if err != nil {
   165→		cclog.Warnf("Error while getting archive tags for job %d in RemoveJobTagByRequest: %v", job, err)
   166→		return nil, err
   167→	}
   168→
   169→	return tags, archive.UpdateTags(j, archiveTags)
   170→}
   171→
   172→// removeTagFromArchiveJobs updates the job archive for all affected jobs after a tag deletion.
   173→//
   174→// This function is called asynchronously (via goroutine) after removing a tag from the database
   175→// to synchronize the file-based job archive with the database state. Errors are logged but not
   176→// returned since this runs in the background.
   177→//
   178→// Parameters:
   179→//   - jobIds: Database IDs of all jobs that had the deleted tag
   180→//
   181→// Implementation note: Each job is processed individually to handle partial failures gracefully.
   182→// If one job fails to update, others will still be processed.
   183→func (r *JobRepository) removeTagFromArchiveJobs(jobIds []int64) {
   184→	for _, j := range jobIds {
   185→		tags, err := r.getArchiveTags(&j)
   186→		if err != nil {
   187→			cclog.Warnf("Error while getting tags for job %d", j)
   188→			continue
   189→		}
   190→
   191→		job, err := r.FindByIDDirect(j)
   192→		if err != nil {
   193→			cclog.Warnf("Error while getting job %d", j)
   194→			continue
   195→		}
   196→
   197→		archive.UpdateTags(job, tags)
   198→	}
   199→}
   200→
   201→// Removes a tag from db by tag info
   202→// Used by REST API. Does not update tagged jobs in Job archive.
   203→func (r *JobRepository) RemoveTagByRequest(tagType string, tagName string, tagScope string) error {
   204→	// Get Tag ID to delete
   205→	tagID, exists := r.TagID(tagType, tagName, tagScope)
   206→	if !exists {
   207→		cclog.Warnf("Tag does not exist (name, type, scope): %s, %s, %s", tagName, tagType, tagScope)
   208→		return fmt.Errorf("tag does not exist (name, type, scope): %s, %s, %s", tagName, tagType, tagScope)
   209→	}
   210→
   211→	return r.RemoveTagByID(tagID)
   212→}
   213→
   214→// Removes a tag from db by tag id
   215→// Used by GraphQL API.
   216→func (r *JobRepository) RemoveTagByID(tagID int64) error {
   217→	jobIds, err := r.FindJobIdsByTag(tagID)
   218→	if err != nil {
   219→		return err
   220→	}
   221→
   222→	// Handle Delete JobTagTable
   223→	qJobTag := sq.Delete("jobtag").Where("jobtag.tag_id = ?", tagID)
   224→
   225→	if _, err := qJobTag.RunWith(r.stmtCache).Exec(); err != nil {
   226→		s, _, _ := qJobTag.ToSql()
   227→		cclog.Errorf("Error removing tag from table 'jobTag' with %s: %v", s, err)
   228→		return err
   229→	}
   230→
   231→	// Handle Delete TagTable
   232→	qTag := sq.Delete("tag").Where("tag.id = ?", tagID)
   233→
   234→	if _, err := qTag.RunWith(r.stmtCache).Exec(); err != nil {
   235→		s, _, _ := qTag.ToSql()
   236→		cclog.Errorf("Error removing tag from table 'tag' with %s: %v", s, err)
   237→		return err
   238→	}
   239→
   240→	// asynchronously update archive jobs
   241→	go r.removeTagFromArchiveJobs(jobIds)
   242→
   243→	return nil
   244→}
   245→
   246→// CreateTag creates a new tag with the specified type, name, and scope.
   247→// Returns the database ID of the newly created tag.
   248→//
   249→// Scope defaults to "global" if empty string is provided.
   250→// Valid scopes: "global", "private", "admin"
   251→//
   252→// Example:
   253→//
   254→//	tagID, err := repo.CreateTag("performance", "high-memory", "global")
   255→func (r *JobRepository) CreateTag(tagType string, tagName string, tagScope string) (tagID int64, err error) {
   256→	// Default to "Global" scope if none defined
   257→	if tagScope == "" {
   258→		tagScope = "global"
   259→	}
   260→
   261→	q := sq.Insert("tag").Columns("tag_type", "tag_name", "tag_scope").Values(tagType, tagName, tagScope)
   262→
   263→	res, err := q.RunWith(r.stmtCache).Exec()
   264→	if err != nil {
   265→		s, _, _ := q.ToSql()
   266→		cclog.Errorf("Error inserting tag with %s: %v", s, err)
   267→		return 0, err
   268→	}
   269→
   270→	return res.LastInsertId()
   271→}
   272→
   273→// CountTags returns all tags visible to the user and the count of jobs for each tag.
   274→// Applies scope-based filtering to respect tag visibility rules.
   275→//
   276→// Returns:
   277→//   - tags: slice of tags the user can see
   278→//   - counts: map of tag name to job count
   279→//   - err: any error encountered
   280→func (r *JobRepository) CountTags(user *schema.User) (tags []schema.Tag, counts map[string]int, err error) {
   281→	tags = make([]schema.Tag, 0, 100)
   282→	xrows, err := r.DB.Queryx("SELECT id, tag_type, tag_name, tag_scope FROM tag")
   283→	if err != nil {
   284→		return nil, nil, err
   285→	}
   286→	defer xrows.Close()
   287→
   288→	for xrows.Next() {
   289→		var t schema.Tag
   290→		if err = xrows.StructScan(&t); err != nil {
   291→			return nil, nil, err
   292→		}
   293→
   294→		// Handle Scope Filtering: Tag Scope is Global, Private (== Username) or User is auth'd to view Admin Tags
   295→		readable, err := r.checkScopeAuth(user, "read", t.Scope)
   296→		if err != nil {
   297→			return nil, nil, err
   298→		}
   299→		if readable {
   300→			tags = append(tags, t)
   301→		}
   302→	}
   303→
   304→	if err := xrows.Err(); err != nil {
   305→		return nil, nil, err
   306→	}
   307→
   308→	// Query and Count Jobs with attached Tags
   309→	q := sq.Select("t.tag_type, t.tag_name, t.id, count(jt.tag_id)").
   310→		From("tag t").
   311→		LeftJoin("jobtag jt ON t.id = jt.tag_id").
   312→		GroupBy("t.tag_type, t.tag_name")
   313→
   314→	// Build scope list for filtering
   315→	var scopeBuilder strings.Builder
   316→	scopeBuilder.WriteString(`"global"`)
   317→	if user != nil {
   318→		scopeBuilder.WriteString(`,"`)
   319→		scopeBuilder.WriteString(user.Username)
   320→		scopeBuilder.WriteString(`"`)
   321→		if user.HasAnyRole([]schema.Role{schema.RoleAdmin, schema.RoleSupport}) {
   322→			scopeBuilder.WriteString(`,"admin"`)
   323→		}
   324→	}
   325→	q = q.Where("t.tag_scope IN (" + scopeBuilder.String() + ")")
   326→
   327→	// Handle Job Ownership
   328→	if user != nil && user.HasAnyRole([]schema.Role{schema.RoleAdmin, schema.RoleSupport}) { // ADMIN || SUPPORT: Count all jobs
   329→		// cclog.Debug("CountTags: User Admin or Support -> Count all Jobs for Tags")
   330→		// Unchanged: Needs to be own case still, due to UserRole/NoRole compatibility handling in else case
   331→	} else if user != nil && user.HasRole(schema.RoleManager) { // MANAGER: Count own jobs plus project's jobs
   332→		// Build ("project1", "project2", ...) list of variable length directly in SQL string
   333→		q = q.Where("jt.job_id IN (SELECT id FROM job WHERE job.hpc_user = ? OR job.project IN (\""+strings.Join(user.Projects, "\",\"")+"\"))", user.Username)
   334→	} else if user != nil { // USER OR NO ROLE (Compatibility): Only count own jobs
   335→		q = q.Where("jt.job_id IN (SELECT id FROM job WHERE job.hpc_user = ?)", user.Username)
   336→	}
   337→
   338→	rows, err := q.RunWith(r.stmtCache).Query()
   339→	if err != nil {
   340→		return nil, nil, err
   341→	}
   342→	defer rows.Close()
   343→
   344→	counts = make(map[string]int)
   345→	for rows.Next() {
   346→		var tagType string
   347→		var tagName string
   348→		var tagID int
   349→		var count int
   350→		if err = rows.Scan(&tagType, &tagName, &tagID, &count); err != nil {
   351→			return nil, nil, err
   352→		}
   353→		// Use tagId as second Map-Key component to differentiate tags with identical names
   354→		counts[fmt.Sprint(tagType, tagName, tagID)] = count
   355→	}
   356→	err = rows.Err()
   357→
   358→	return tags, counts, err
   359→}
   360→
   361→var (
   362→	// ErrTagNotFound is returned when a tag ID or tag identifier (type, name, scope) does not exist in the database.
   363→	ErrTagNotFound = errors.New("the tag does not exist")
   364→
   365→	// ErrJobNotOwned is returned when a user attempts to tag a job they do not have permission to access.
   366→	ErrJobNotOwned = errors.New("user is not owner of job")
   367→
   368→	// ErrTagNoAccess is returned when a user attempts to use a tag they cannot access due to scope restrictions.
   369→	ErrTagNoAccess = errors.New("user not permitted to use that tag")
   370→
   371→	// ErrTagPrivateScope is returned when a user attempts to access another user's private tag.
   372→	ErrTagPrivateScope = errors.New("tag is private to another user")
   373→
   374→	// ErrTagAdminScope is returned when a non-admin user attempts to use an admin-scoped tag.
   375→	ErrTagAdminScope = errors.New("tag requires admin privileges")
   376→
   377→	// ErrTagsIncompatScopes is returned when attempting to combine admin and non-admin scoped tags in a single operation.
   378→	ErrTagsIncompatScopes = errors.New("combining admin and non-admin scoped tags not allowed")
   379→)

   381→// addJobTag is a helper function that inserts a job-tag association and updates the archive.
   382→//
   383→// This function performs three operations atomically:
   384→//  1. Inserts the job-tag association into the jobtag junction table
   385→//  2. Retrieves the updated tag list for the job (using the provided getTags callback)
   386→//  3. Updates the job archive with the new tags to maintain database-archive consistency
   387→//
   388→// Parameters:
   389→//   - jobId: Database ID of the job
   390→//   - tagId: Database ID of the tag to associate
   391→//   - job: Full job object needed for archive update
   392→//   - getTags: Callback function to retrieve updated tags (allows different security contexts)
   393→//
   394→// Returns the complete updated tag list for the job or an error.
   395→//
   396→// Note: This function does NOT validate tag scope permissions - callers must perform
   397→// authorization checks before invoking this helper.
   398→func (r *JobRepository) addJobTag(jobID int64, tagID int64, job *schema.Job, getTags func() ([]*schema.Tag, error)) ([]*schema.Tag, error) {
   399→	q := sq.Insert("jobtag").Columns("job_id", "tag_id").Values(jobID, tagID)
   400→
   401→	if _, err := q.RunWith(r.stmtCache).Exec(); err != nil {
   402→		s, _, _ := q.ToSql()
   403→		cclog.Errorf("Error adding tag with %s: %v", s, err)
   404→		return nil, err
   405→	}
   406→
   407→	tags, err := getTags()
   408→	if err != nil {
   409→		cclog.Warnf("Error getting tags for job %d: %v", jobID, err)
   410→		return nil, err
   411→	}
   412→
   413→	archiveTags, err := r.getArchiveTags(&jobID)
   414→	if err != nil {
   415→		cclog.Warnf("Error getting archive tags for job %d: %v", jobID, err)
   416→		return nil, err
   417→	}
   418→
   419→	return tags, archive.UpdateTags(job, archiveTags)
   420→}
   421→
   422→// AddTagOrCreate adds the tag with the specified type and name to the job with the database id `jobId`.
   423→// If such a tag does not yet exist, it is created.
   424→func (r *JobRepository) AddTagOrCreate(user *schema.User, jobID int64, tagType string, tagName string, tagScope string) (tagID int64, err error) {
   425→	// Default to "Global" scope if none defined
   426→	if tagScope == "" {
   427→		tagScope = "global"
   428→	}
   429→
   430→	writable, err := r.checkScopeAuth(user, "write", tagScope)
   431→	if err != nil {
   432→		return 0, err
   433→	}
   434→	if !writable {
   435→		return 0, fmt.Errorf("cannot write tag scope with current authorization")
   436→	}
   437→
   438→	tagID, exists := r.TagID(tagType, tagName, tagScope)
   439→	if !exists {
   440→		tagID, err = r.CreateTag(tagType, tagName, tagScope)
   441→		if err != nil {
   442→			return 0, err
   443→		}
   444→	}
   445→
   446→	if _, err := r.AddTag(user, jobID, tagID); err != nil {
   447→		return 0, err
   448→	}
   449→
   450→	return tagID, nil
   451→}
   452→
   453→func (r *JobRepository) AddTagOrCreateDirect(jobID int64, tagType string, tagName string) (tagID int64, err error) {
   454→	tagScope := "global"
   455→
   456→	tagID, exists := r.TagID(tagType, tagName, tagScope)
   457→	if !exists {
   458→		tagID, err = r.CreateTag(tagType, tagName, tagScope)
   459→		if err != nil {
   460→			return 0, err
   461→		}
   462→	}
   463→
   464→	cclog.Infof("Adding tag %s:%s:%s (direct)", tagType, tagName, tagScope)
   465→
   466→	if _, err := r.AddTagDirect(jobID, tagID); err != nil {
   467→		return 0, err
   468→	}
   469→
   470→	return tagID, nil
   471→}

   473→func (r *JobRepository) HasTag(jobID int64, tagType string, tagName string) bool {
   474→	var id int64
   475→	q := sq.Select("id").From("tag").Join("jobtag ON jobtag.tag_id = tag.id").
   476→		Where("jobtag.job_id = ?", jobID).Where("tag.tag_type = ?", tagType).
   477→		Where("tag.tag_name = ?", tagName)
   478→	err := q.RunWith(r.stmtCache).QueryRow().Scan(&id)
   479→	if err != nil {
   480→		return false
   481→	} else {
   482→		return true
   483→	}
   484→}

   507→// GetTags returns a list of all scoped tags if job is nil or of the tags that the job with that database ID has.
   508→func (r *JobRepository) GetTags(user *schema.User, job *int64) ([]*schema.Tag, error) {
   509→	q := sq.Select("id", "tag_type", "tag_name", "tag_scope").From("tag")
   510→	if job != nil {
   511→		q = q.Join("jobtag ON jobtag.tag_id = tag.id").Where("jobtag.job_id = ?", *job)
   512→	}
   513→
   514→	rows, err := q.RunWith(r.stmtCache).Query()
   515→	if err != nil {
   516→		s, _, _ := q.ToSql()
   517→		cclog.Errorf("Error get tags with %s: %v", s, err)
   518→		return nil, err
   519→	}
   520→	defer rows.Close()
   521→
   522→	tags := make([]*schema.Tag, 0)
   523→	for rows.Next() {
   524→		tag := &schema.Tag{}
   525→		if err := rows.Scan(&tag.ID, &tag.Type, &tag.Name, &tag.Scope); err != nil {
   526→			cclog.Warnf("Error while scanning tag rows in GetTags: %v", err)
   527→			return nil, err
   528→		}
   529→		// Handle Scope Filtering: Tag Scope is Global, Private (== Username) or User is auth'd to view Admin Tags
   530→		readable, err := r.checkScopeAuth(user, "read", tag.Scope)
   531→		if err != nil {
   532→			return nil, err
   533→		}
   534→		if readable {
   535→			tags = append(tags, tag)
   536→		}
   537→	}
   538→
   539→	if err := rows.Err(); err != nil {
   540→		return nil, err
   541→	}
   542→
   543→	return tags, nil
   544→}

   620→func (r *JobRepository) ImportTag(jobID int64, tagType string, tagName string, tagScope string) (err error) {
   621→	// Import has no scope ctx, only import from metafile to DB (No recursive archive update required), only returns err
   622→
   623→	tagID, exists := r.TagID(tagType, tagName, tagScope)
   624→	if !exists {
   625→		tagID, err = r.CreateTag(tagType, tagName, tagScope)
   626→		if err != nil {
   627→			return err
   628→		}
   629→	}
   630→
   631→	q := sq.Insert("jobtag").Columns("job_id", "tag_id").Values(jobID, tagID)
   632→
   633→	if _, err := q.RunWith(r.stmtCache).Exec(); err != nil {
   634→		s, _, _ := q.ToSql()
   635→		cclog.Errorf("Error adding tag on import with %s: %v", s, err)
   636→		return err
   637→	}
   638→
   639→	return nil
   640→}

   642→// checkScopeAuth validates whether a user is authorized to perform an operation on a tag with the given scope.
   643→//
   644→// This function implements the tag scope authorization matrix:
   645→//
   646→//	Scope        | Read Access                      | Write Access
   647→//	-------------|----------------------------------|----------------------------------
   648→//	"global"     | All users                        | Admin, Support, API-only
   649→//	"admin"      | Admin, Support                   | Admin, API-only
   650→//	<username>   | Owner only                       | Owner only (private tags)
   651→//
   652→// Parameters:
   653→//   - user: User attempting the operation (must not be nil)
   654→//   - operation: Either "read" or "write"
   655→//   - scope: Tag scope value ("global", "admin", or username for private tags)
   656→//
   657→// Returns:
   658→//   - pass: true if authorized, false if denied
   659→//   - err: error only if operation is invalid or user is nil
   660→//
   661→// Special cases:
   662→//   - API-only users (single role: RoleApi) can write to admin and global scopes for automation
   663→//   - Private tags use the username as scope, granting exclusive access to that user
   664→func (r *JobRepository) checkScopeAuth(user *schema.User, operation string, scope string) (pass bool, err error) {
   665→	if user != nil {
   666→		switch {
   667→		case operation == "write" && scope == "admin":
   668→			if user.HasRole(schema.RoleAdmin) || (len(user.Roles) == 1 && user.HasRole(schema.RoleAPI)) {
   669→				return true, nil
   670→			}
   671→			return false, nil
   672→		case operation == "write" && scope == "global":
   673→			if user.HasAnyRole([]schema.Role{schema.RoleAdmin, schema.RoleSupport}) || (len(user.Roles) == 1 && user.HasRole(schema.RoleAPI)) {
   674→				return true, nil
   675→			}
   676→			return false, nil
   677→		case operation == "write" && scope == user.Username:
   678→			return true, nil
   679→		case operation == "read" && scope == "admin":
   680→			return user.HasAnyRole([]schema.Role{schema.RoleAdmin, schema.RoleSupport}), nil
   681→		case operation == "read" && scope == "global":
   682→			return true, nil
   683→		case operation == "read" && scope == user.Username:
   684→			return true, nil
   685→		default:
   686→			if operation == "read" || operation == "write" {
   687→				// No acceptable scope: deny tag
   688→				return false, nil
   689→			} else {
   690→				return false, fmt.Errorf("error while checking tag operation auth: unknown operation (%s)", operation)
   691→			}
   692→		}
   693→	} else {
   694→		return false, fmt.Errorf("error while checking tag operation auth: no user in context")
   695→	}
   696→}

    17→const NamedJobCacheInsert string = `INSERT INTO job_cache (
    18→	job_id, hpc_user, project, cluster, subcluster, cluster_partition, array_job_id, num_nodes, num_hwthreads, num_acc,
    19→	shared, monitoring_status, smt, job_state, start_time, duration, walltime, footprint, energy, energy_footprint, resources, meta_data
    20→) VALUES (
    21→	:job_id, :hpc_user, :project, :cluster, :subcluster, :cluster_partition, :array_job_id, :num_nodes, :num_hwthreads, :num_acc,
    22→  :shared, :monitoring_status, :smt, :job_state, :start_time, :duration, :walltime, :footprint,  :energy, :energy_footprint, :resources, :meta_data
    23→);`
    24→
    25→const NamedJobInsert string = `INSERT INTO job (
    26→	job_id, hpc_user, project, cluster, subcluster, cluster_partition, array_job_id, num_nodes, num_hwthreads, num_acc,
    27→	shared, monitoring_status, smt, job_state, start_time, duration, walltime, footprint, energy, energy_footprint, resources, meta_data
    28→) VALUES (
    29→	:job_id, :hpc_user, :project, :cluster, :subcluster, :cluster_partition, :array_job_id, :num_nodes, :num_hwthreads, :num_acc,
    30→  :shared, :monitoring_status, :smt, :job_state, :start_time, :duration, :walltime, :footprint,  :energy, :energy_footprint, :resources, :meta_data
    31→);`
    32→
    33→// InsertJobDirect inserts a job directly into the job table (not job_cache).
    34→// Use this when the returned ID will be used for operations on the job table
    35→// (e.g., adding tags), or for imported jobs that are already completed.
    36→func (r *JobRepository) InsertJobDirect(job *schema.Job) (int64, error) {
    37→	r.Mutex.Lock()
    38→	defer r.Mutex.Unlock()
    39→
    40→	res, err := r.DB.NamedExec(NamedJobInsert, job)
    41→	if err != nil {
    42→		cclog.Warn("Error while NamedJobInsert (direct)")
    43→		return 0, err
    44→	}
    45→	id, err := res.LastInsertId()
    46→	if err != nil {
    47→		cclog.Warn("Error while getting last insert ID (direct)")
    48→		return 0, err
    49→	}
    50→
    51→	return id, nil
    52→}
    53→
    54→func (r *JobRepository) InsertJob(job *schema.Job) (int64, error) {
    55→	r.Mutex.Lock()
    56→	defer r.Mutex.Unlock()
    57→
    58→	res, err := r.DB.NamedExec(NamedJobCacheInsert, job)
    59→	if err != nil {
    60→		cclog.Warn("Error while NamedJobInsert")
    61→		return 0, err
    62→	}
    63→	id, err := res.LastInsertId()
    64→	if err != nil {
    65→		cclog.Warn("Error while getting last insert ID")
    66→		return 0, err
    67→	}
    68→
    69→	return id, nil
    70→}

    72→func (r *JobRepository) SyncJobs() ([]*schema.Job, error) {
    73→	r.Mutex.Lock()
    74→	defer r.Mutex.Unlock()
    75→
    76→	query := sq.Select(jobCacheColumns...).From("job_cache")
    77→
    78→	rows, err := query.RunWith(r.stmtCache).Query()
    79→	if err != nil {
    80→		cclog.Errorf("Error while running query %v", err)
    81→		return nil, err
    82→	}
    83→	defer rows.Close()
    84→
    85→	jobs := make([]*schema.Job, 0, 50)
    86→	for rows.Next() {
    87→		job, err := scanJob(rows)
    88→		if err != nil {
    89→			cclog.Warn("Error while scanning rows")
    90→			return nil, err
    91→		}
    92→		jobs = append(jobs, job)
    93→	}
    94→
    95→	// Transfer cached jobs to main table and clear cache in a single transaction.
    96→	tx, err := r.DB.Beginx()
    97→	if err != nil {
    98→		cclog.Errorf("SyncJobs: begin transaction: %v", err)
    99→		return nil, err
   100→	}
   101→	defer tx.Rollback()
   102→
   103→	// Use INSERT OR IGNORE to skip jobs already transferred by the stop path
   104→	_, err = tx.Exec(
   105→		"INSERT OR IGNORE INTO job (job_id, cluster, subcluster, start_time, hpc_user, project, cluster_partition, array_job_id, num_nodes, num_hwthreads, num_acc, shared, monitoring_status, smt, job_state, duration, walltime, footprint, energy, energy_footprint, resources, meta_data) SELECT job_id, cluster, subcluster, start_time, hpc_user, project, cluster_partition, array_job_id, num_nodes, num_hwthreads, num_acc, shared, monitoring_status, smt, job_state, duration, walltime, footprint, energy, energy_footprint, resources, meta_data FROM job_cache")
   106→	if err != nil {
   107→		cclog.Errorf("Error while Job sync: %v", err)
   108→		return nil, err
   109→	}
   110→
   111→	_, err = tx.Exec("DELETE FROM job_cache")
   112→	if err != nil {
   113→		cclog.Errorf("Error while Job cache clean: %v", err)
   114→		return nil, err
   115→	}
   116→
   117→	if err := tx.Commit(); err != nil {
   118→		cclog.Errorf("SyncJobs: commit transaction: %v", err)
   119→		return nil, err
   120→	}
   121→
   122→	// Resolve correct job.id from the job table. The IDs read from job_cache
   123→	// are from a different auto-increment sequence and must not be used to
   124→	// query the job table.
   125→	for _, job := range jobs {
   126→		var newID int64
   127→		if err := sq.Select("job.id").From("job").
   128→			Where("job.job_id = ? AND job.cluster = ? AND job.start_time = ?",
   129→				job.JobID, job.Cluster, job.StartTime).
   130→			RunWith(r.stmtCache).QueryRow().Scan(&newID); err != nil {
   131→			cclog.Warnf("SyncJobs: could not resolve job table id for job %d on %s: %v",
   132→				job.JobID, job.Cluster, err)
   133→			continue
   134→		}
   135→		job.ID = &newID
   136→	}
   137→
   138→	return jobs, nil
   139→}
   140→
   141→// TransferCachedJobToMain moves a job from job_cache to the job table.
   142→// Caller must hold r.Mutex. Returns the new job table ID.
   143→func (r *JobRepository) TransferCachedJobToMain(cacheID int64) (int64, error) {
   144→	res, err := r.DB.Exec(
   145→		"INSERT INTO job (job_id, cluster, subcluster, start_time, hpc_user, project, cluster_partition, array_job_id, num_nodes, num_hwthreads, num_acc, shared, monitoring_status, smt, job_state, duration, walltime, footprint, energy, energy_footprint, resources, meta_data) SELECT job_id, cluster, subcluster, start_time, hpc_user, project, cluster_partition, array_job_id, num_nodes, num_hwthreads, num_acc, shared, monitoring_status, smt, job_state, duration, walltime, footprint, energy, energy_footprint, resources, meta_data FROM job_cache WHERE id = ?",
   146→		cacheID)
   147→	if err != nil {
   148→		return 0, fmt.Errorf("transferring cached job %d to main table failed: %w", cacheID, err)
   149→	}
   150→
   151→	newID, err := res.LastInsertId()
   152→	if err != nil {
   153→		return 0, fmt.Errorf("getting new job ID after transfer failed: %w", err)
   154→	}
   155→
   156→	_, err = r.DB.Exec("DELETE FROM job_cache WHERE id = ?", cacheID)
   157→	if err != nil {
   158→		return 0, fmt.Errorf("deleting cached job %d after transfer failed: %w", cacheID, err)
   159→	}
   160→
   161→	return newID, nil
   162→}
   163→
   164→// Start inserts a new job in the table, returning the unique job ID.
   165→// Statistics are not transfered!
   166→func (r *JobRepository) Start(job *schema.Job) (id int64, err error) {
   167→	job.RawFootprint, err = json.Marshal(job.Footprint)
   168→	if err != nil {
   169→		return -1, fmt.Errorf("REPOSITORY/JOB > encoding footprint field failed: %w", err)
   170→	}
   171→
   172→	job.RawResources, err = json.Marshal(job.Resources)
   173→	if err != nil {
   174→		return -1, fmt.Errorf("REPOSITORY/JOB > encoding resources field failed: %w", err)
   175→	}
   176→
   177→	job.RawMetaData, err = json.Marshal(job.MetaData)
   178→	if err != nil {
   179→		return -1, fmt.Errorf("REPOSITORY/JOB > encoding metaData field failed: %w", err)
   180→	}
   181→
   182→	return r.InsertJob(job)
   183→}
   184→
   185→// StartDirect inserts a new job directly into the job table (not job_cache).
   186→// Use this when the returned ID will immediately be used for job table
   187→// operations such as adding tags.
   188→func (r *JobRepository) StartDirect(job *schema.Job) (id int64, err error) {
   189→	job.RawFootprint, err = json.Marshal(job.Footprint)
   190→	if err != nil {
   191→		return -1, fmt.Errorf("REPOSITORY/JOB > encoding footprint field failed: %w", err)
   192→	}
   193→
   194→	job.RawResources, err = json.Marshal(job.Resources)
   195→	if err != nil {
   196→		return -1, fmt.Errorf("REPOSITORY/JOB > encoding resources field failed: %w", err)
   197→	}
   198→
   199→	job.RawMetaData, err = json.Marshal(job.MetaData)
   200→	if err != nil {
   201→		return -1, fmt.Errorf("REPOSITORY/JOB > encoding metaData field failed: %w", err)
   202→	}
   203→
   204→	return r.InsertJobDirect(job)
   205→}
   206→
   207→// Stop updates the job with the database id jobId using the provided arguments.
   208→func (r *JobRepository) Stop(
   209→	jobID int64,
   210→	duration int32,
   211→	state schema.JobState,
   212→	monitoringStatus int32,
   213→) (err error) {
   214→	// Invalidate cache entries as job state is changing
   215→	r.cache.Del(fmt.Sprintf("metadata:%d", jobID))
   216→	r.cache.Del(fmt.Sprintf("energyFootprint:%d", jobID))
   217→
   218→	stmt := sq.Update("job").
   219→		Set("job_state", state).
   220→		Set("duration", duration).
   221→		Set("monitoring_status", monitoringStatus).
   222→		Where("job.id = ?", jobID)
   223→
   224→	_, err = stmt.RunWith(r.stmtCache).Exec()
   225→	return err
   226→}
